sources: Avoma transcript, 19 Aug 2025 demo (speaker: Amresh Munshi)
metrics.narrative: On the 19 Aug 2025 demo, Amresh Munshi set concrete negotiation parameters: a savings target of 4-9% on autonomous negotiations, a sub-$25,000 autonomous-sourcing threshold (currently informally raised to $50,000 but targeted back down to $25,000), a 3-5% cap on annual renewal increases, and standard payment terms (Net 60 new vendors, push existing vendors toward Net 45). These are real, buyer-articulated figures, not rep assumptions. However, no formal ROI workshop or savings model has been built since, and none of this has been reconfirmed by the buyer in the ~11 months since that call.

champion.narrative: Amresh Munshi acted as a genuine champion on the 19 Aug 2025 and 13 Jan 2026 calls — he set the agenda, defined evaluation criteria, and committed to bringing in procurement ops, power users, and the (unnamed) Head of Global Procurement. But he has missed his own commitments repeatedly: the September 2025 follow-up with Chris Soriano and Alycia Stafford never happened as promised (it slipped to a January 2026 demo instead), a promised early-March 2026 check-in (his own request, per his 26 Feb 2026 reply) never materialized, and there has been no verified reply from him since that 26 Feb 2026 email despite at least 6 documented outreach attempts (9 Mar, 12 Mar, 25 Mar, 7 Apr, 13 May, 22 Jun, 29 Jun 2026). No other buyer contact (Purav Desai, Chris Soriano, Alycia Stafford, Justin Robinson) has independently engaged beyond being cc'd or invited.

sources: Avoma transcript, 19 Aug 2025 demo (speaker: Amresh Munshi)
competition.narrative: Amresh Munshi confirmed a live multi-vendor bake-off on the 19 Aug 2025 call: 'we are exploring all the providers to see who can best fit our requirement, and the player who is having more capability... will onboard one or two of them to go for the POC.' Salesforce lists GEP Worldwide, Ivalua, JAGGAER and SAP Ariba as named competitors. Cornerstone's own decision window (finalize by Sept 2025, POC Dec 2025/Jan 2026) has passed with zero subsequent competitive intelligence — we do not know whether Zycus is still in contention.

sources: Avoma transcript, 19 Aug 2025 demo (speaker: Amresh Munshi)
identify_pain.narrative: Amresh Munshi articulated detailed, specific pain on the 19 Aug 2025 call: tail-spend purchases (under $20-25k) are handled entirely outside any system via phone/email with no audit trail; Oracle iProcurement (tied to the relevant stakeholder) cannot be replaced but is limited and costly to enhance, a legacy of a 2021 leadership decision to move off NetSuite/Coupa onto Oracle; and a recent ~$10,000 software purchase bypassed a mandatory TSR security review, creating a real compliance incident. This is rich, buyer-voiced pain, though it has not been reconfirmed in the ~11 months since that call.

paper_process.narrative: No commercials, pricing, MSA, or SOW discussion has started. The Amount field has moved several times ($120k created → $210k on 4 Sep 2025 → $185k on 18 Mar 2026 current) but these appear to be internal scoping adjustments, not buyer-negotiated commercial terms — no call or email in the record discusses price with the buyer.

economic_buyer.narrative: Amresh Munshi named the Economic Buyer role explicitly on the 19 Aug 2025 call: 'finally, my head of global procurement to be part of it to give his blessing and take it forward.' That person has never been named, never joined a call, and no budget or approval conversation has started at any point in the 11-month history of this deal per Salesforce field history and Next Step History.

sources: Avoma transcript, 19 Aug 2025 demo (speaker: Amresh Munshi)

decision_process.narrative: Amresh Munshi laid out a detailed process on the 19 Aug 2025 call: exploration through Sept 2025, vendor identification/pricing after Sept, POC with one or two finalists by Dec 2025/Jan 2026, contracting during and just after the POC, and a targeted go-live of May 2026 (with an internal 'safer' target of roughly a year out). Every one of those milestones has since passed without confirmed resolution — the POC decision point (Dec 2025/Jan 2026) came and went with only a third demo held 13 Jan 2026, and no RFP, POC, or contracting activity has been logged since.

sources: Next_Step_History__c entry 2025-12-01 (Steve Ovadje): 'Cornerstone team is planning to send out a structured RFI by end of month'
decision_criteria.narrative: Amresh Munshi described specific evaluation needs on the 19 Aug 2025 call: strategic sourcing analytics with pre-negotiation insights and historical benchmarking for high-value SaaS/platform deals, configurable negotiation tone/aggressiveness, and a hard requirement that any new software purchase (regardless of dollar threshold) trigger an IT security (TSR) review given a past internal security incident. A structured RFI was reportedly being prepared by Cornerstone in Dec 2025 but no evidence confirms it was ever sent or received.

competitive_position.summary: A multi-vendor bake-off was confirmed by the buyer on 19 Aug 2025 (GEP Worldwide, Ivalua, JAGGAER, SAP Ariba named in Salesforce), with a POC-selection decision point originally targeted for Dec 2025/Jan 2026. That window has passed with zero subsequent competitive intelligence. Oracle iProcurement is a fixed incumbent that cannot be replaced (tied to Oracle Fusion ERP) and Zycus's proposal is explicitly an overlay, not a replacement.

hard.ais_why:
• Champion Amresh Munshi engaged the August 19 2025 demo squarely on autonomous AI. The call confirmed strong interest in autonomous sourcing and AI-enabled negotiation, with explicit configurable thresholds for purchases below $25,000. He set savings expectations of 4 to 9 percent from autonomous negotiation, framing AI as the value driver rather than a feature on the wishlist.
• Amresh and ops lead Purav Desai treated AI as the core architecture. The integration requirement was that Zycus deliver an overlay solution on Oracle iProcurement, Oracle Fusion and ServiceNow without ripping iProcurement, with mandatory TSR review automation for sub-$25K critical software. That is buyer-led specification of where AI must plug in.
• Project sequencing reinforces the Hungry call: exploration through September, POC in December or January, go-live targeted May 2026 within roughly a year. Amresh is convening additional stakeholders including Chris and Alicia and power users. Two recorded sessions, one completed and one cancelled, plus action items committing both sides to AI-anchored next steps.
